sound_type: roaring bark -- deep, sustained, thunderous colony roar. Bulls on breeding rookeries. Loudest PNW pinniped. E=6
ecosystem: Rocky haul-out sites, offshore islands, exposed coastline. Breeding rookeries on sea stacks and offshore islands. Resident in PNW.
season: Year-round resident. Breeding: May-July on rookeries. Non-breeding haul-outs year-round.

coordinates.description: Steller Sea Lion on outer Washington coast / offshore islands. Rookeries and haul-outs on rocky sites.

taxonomy_note.description: FIRST OTARIIDAE (eared seals) in the engine. Largest sea lion in the world. Named after Georg Wilhelm Steller (1741 Bering expedition). Endangered (western DPS). Three Otariidae entries planned (182-184).
taxonomy_note.family_count_in_engine: Otariidae: Steller Sea Lion (182) -- FIRST. California Sea Lion (183) and Northern Fur Seal (184) upcoming.
taxonomy_note.name_etymology: Eumetopias from Greek 'eu' (true/good) + 'metopon' (forehead) -- broad forehead. jubatus from Latin 'jubatus' (maned) -- the thick neck mane of males. Named for Georg Wilhelm Steller (1709-1746).
taxonomy_note.genus_note: Eumetopias -- monotypic genus. Only species: E. jubatus. The sole member of its genus.
taxonomy_note.order_note: Carnivora. Second Carnivora in SPS (after Harbor Seal, 180).
taxonomy_note.family_position: First Otariidae. The eared seal family enters, distinguished from Phocidae by external ears, rotating flippers, fore-flipper swimming.
taxonomy_note.otariidae_vs_phocidae: Otariidae: external ear flaps, rotate hind flippers, walk on land, swim with fore flippers. Phocidae: no external ears, cannot rotate hind flippers, undulate on land, swim with hind flippers.

song_metrics.fundamental_frequency_range_hz: 100-3000 Hz. Deep fundamental with rich harmonics.
song_metrics.song_duration_s: Individual roar: 1-5 seconds. Repeated in series. Continuous at rookeries.
song_metrics.note_structure: Deep, sustained, thunderous roar. Belching quality. Also: cow calls (shorter, lower), pup bleats (higher).
song_metrics.note_types: Bull roar (primary), cow call, pup bleat, colony cacophony.
song_metrics.phrase_structure: Roar series: multiple calls in rapid succession during territorial disputes. Colony: overlapping continuous vocalizations.
song_metrics.timbre: Deep, resonant, thunderous. Chest resonance amplified by massive body. Intimidating.
song_metrics.amplitude: E=6. Maximum for SPS. Loudest PNW pinniped. Carries across rookery and beyond.
song_metrics.tempo: Roar rate variable: rapid during disputes, intermittent during patrol.
song_metrics.context: Breeding rookeries, territorial defense, dominance, haul-outs.
song_metrics.regional_variation: Minimal. Individual recognition by colony members.
song_metrics.key_property: COLONY ROAR. FIRST OTARIIDAE. Largest sea lion. 2,500 lbs. Endangered (western DPS). E=6 maximum. v1.49.323.

pelage.overall: Light tan to reddish-brown. Males darker, with thick mane of coarse hair around neck. Females lighter.
pelage.diagnostic_mark: Massive size (males). Thick neck mane. External ear flaps (vs Phocidae). Broad forehead. Can walk on all four flippers.
pelage.seasonal_variation: Minimal. Males develop fuller mane with maturity.
pelage.sexual_dimorphism: Extreme. Males: 3.3m, 1,120 kg. Females: 2.5m, 350 kg. 3:1 weight ratio.

physical_description.overall: Males: length 3.3m, weight up to 1,120 kg. Females: 2.5m, up to 350 kg. Massive forequarters, thick neck, sagittal crest (males). External ear flaps.
physical_description.diagnostic_mark: Largest sea lion. External ear flaps. Broad forehead. Thick neck mane (males). Walks on flippers.
physical_description.field_id: Very large sea lion on rocky haul-out. Tan/reddish-brown. External ear flaps visible. Males enormous with thick neck. Listen for deep, sustained roar.

nodes.title: First Otariidae -- Eared Seals Enter
nodes.description: FIRST OTARIIDAE in the engine. Eared seal family distinguished from Phocidae by external ears, rotating flippers, fore-flipper swimming. Three entries: 182-184.

nodes.title: Colony Roar -- E=6 Maximum
nodes.description: Loudest PNW pinniped. Deep, sustained, thunderous bull roar. Colony cacophony at breeding rookeries. 100-3000 Hz with rich harmonics.

nodes.title: Largest Sea Lion -- 2,500 lbs
nodes.description: Males up to 3.3m, 1,120 kg. 3x female weight. Extreme sexual dimorphism. Megafauna scale. Named for Georg Steller (1741).

nodes.title: Endangered -- Conservation Crisis
nodes.description: Western DPS: ESA Endangered (~80% decline). Eastern DPS: delisted 2013 after recovery. Nutritional stress, fisheries, predation, historical harvest.

cross_references.relationship: PHOCIDAE vs OTARIIDAE! True seal vs eared seal. The fundamental pinniped split. Bark (E=4) vs roar (E=6). No ears vs external ears.
OTARIIDAE PAIR! Steller (182) and California (183): comparison pair. Largest vs most familiar. Same family, different scale.
OTARIIDAE TRIO! Third and final eared seal. Fur seal vs sea lion within the family. Different ecological niche.
RAW POWER! Gangsta rap confrontation + colony roar. E=7 + E=6 = E=13. Highest combined energy in the engine.
